title.value: StepSearch: Igniting LLMs Search Ability via Step-Wise Proximal Policy Optimization
title.evidence: StepSearch: Igniting LLMs Search Ability via Step-Wise Proximal Policy Optimization

multihop_reasoning_challenge.value: Efficient multi-hop reasoning requires Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents to acquire high-value external knowledge iteratively.
multihop_reasoning_challenge.evidence: Efficient multi-hop reasoning requires Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents to acquire high-value external knowledge iteratively.

sparse_reward_problem.value: Previous work has explored reinforcement learning (RL) to train LLMs to perform search-based document retrieval, achieving notable improvements in QA performance, but underperform on complex, multi-hop QA resulting from the sparse rewards from global signal only.
sparse_reward_problem.evidence: Previous work has explored reinforcement learning (RL) to train LLMs to perform search-based document retrieval, achieving notable improvements in QA performance, but underperform on complex, multi-hop QA resulting from the sparse rewards from global signal only.

search_efficiency_problem.value: Complex multi-hop QA still suffers from intrinsic knowledge gaps and static, inefficient knowledge-assimilation mechanisms.
search_efficiency_problem.evidence: However, complex multi-hop QA still suffers from intrinsic knowledge gaps and static, inefficient knowledge-assimilation mechanisms.

existing_method_performance.value: Existing RL-based search agents depend on coarse global rewards, lacking fine-grained supervision of intermediate queries and multi-step retrievals—an approach inadequate for the dependencies inherent in complex multi-hop reasoning.
existing_method_performance.evidence: However, existing RL-based search agents depend on coarse global rewards, lacking fine-grained supervision of intermediate queries and multi-step retrievals—an approach inadequate for the dependencies inherent in complex multi-hop reasoning.

framework_overview.value: StepSearch is a framework for search LLMs trained with step-wise proximal policy optimization method, consisting of richer and more detailed intermediate search rewards and token-level process supervision based on information gain and redundancy penalties to better guide each search step.
framework_overview.evidence: To address this gap in existing research, we introduce StepSearch, a framework for search LLMs that trained with step-wise proximal policy optimization method. It consists of richer and more detailed intermediate search rewards and token-level process supervision based on information gain and redundancy penalties to better guide each search step.

subproblem_decomposition.value: Leverage GPT-4o to enrich decomposed MuSiQue questions with coherent subquestion–answer pairs, then derive N search queries per step for retrieval.
subproblem_decomposition.evidence: (a) Leverage GPT-4o. to enrich decomposed MuSiQue questions with coherent subquestion–answer pairs, then derive N search queries per step for retrieval.

search_keyword_generation.value: Each enhanced step question is then reformulated into a set of N search queries to facilitate information retrieval.
search_keyword_generation.evidence: (b) Each enhanced step question is then reformulated into a set of N search queries to facilitate information retrieval.

cross_engine_validation.value: Queries are issued to M sources (e.g., Google, Bing, Wiki-18), and only those returning valid results in at least ⌈M / 2⌉ sources are retained.
cross_engine_validation.evidence: (c) Queries are issued to M sources (e.g., Google, Bing, Wiki-18), and only those returning valid results in at least ⌈M / 2⌉ sources are retained.

dataset_scale.value: We develop a novel MuSiQue-based pipeline, contributing 60k filtered sub-question search keywords.
dataset_scale.evidence: We develop a novel MuSiQue-based pipeline, contributing 60k filtered sub-question search keywords that generalize across retrieval datasets.

global_rewards.value: The global reward is based on final answer accuracy and keyword hit rate, applied at the last token.
global_rewards.evidence: Search Step Reward score, combining information gain and redundancy penalty, are applied to tokens within each round, while the global reward, based on final answer accuracy and keyword hit rate, is applied at the last token.

step_rewards.value: Each token earns an information-gain signal G^t and incurs a redundancy penalty P^t.
step_rewards.evidence: Our method further diverges from vanilla PPO by segmenting each turn into think search answer phases and assigning token-level rewards: each token earns an information-gain signal G^t and incurs a redundancy penalty P^t.

algorithm_basis.value: The optimization algorithm combines Search Steps supervision reward based on the currently widely used actor-critic approach PPO, denoted as StePPO.
algorithm_basis.evidence: Our optimization algorithm combines the above-mentioned Search Steps supervision reward based on the currently widely used actor-critic approach PPO, denote as StePPO.

core_mechanism.value: StePPO uses token-level rewards combining information gain and redundancy penalties to boost policy convergence and improve retrieval fidelity and QA accuracy.
core_mechanism.evidence: By augmenting PPO with token-level rewards that combine information gain and redundancy penalties, StepSearch boosts policy convergence and improves retrieval fidelity and QA accuracy.

mathematical_formulation.value: The mathematical formulation involves optimizing the policy π_θ with the reference policy π_θ_ref by maximizing the objective T_StePPO(θ) using token-loss masking indicators.
mathematical_formulation.evidence: Then optimize the policy π_θ with the reference policy π_θ_ref by maximizing the following objective: T_StePPO(θ) = E_{x~D, o~π_{θ_old}(·|x)} { 1 / ∑_{t=1}^{|σ|} I(o_t) } ∑_{t=1: I(o_t)=1}^{|ω|} [ π_θ(o_t | x, o_<t) / π_{θ_old}(o_t | x, o_<t) A_t, clip( π_θ(o_t | x, o_<t) / π_{θ_old}(o_t | x, o_<t), 1-ε, 1+ε) A_t ]

performance_improvements.value: StepSearch consistently outperforms strong baseline Search-RL methods, showing robust generalization and adaptability to out-of-domain knowledge bases.
performance_improvements.evidence: StepSearch consistently outperforms strong baseline Search-RL methods. Our method performance advantage holds for both in-domain multi-hop (i.e., MuSiQue) and out-ofdomain (i.e., HotpotQA, 2WikiMultiHopQA, and Bamboogle) datasets, demonstrating the robustness of our method.

data_efficiency.value: Using only a knowledge base with a smaller amount of retrieval database (about 0.35%) and training data (about 11%) can show even better adaptability compared to models trained on larger datasets.
data_efficiency.evidence: Using only a knowledge base with a smaller amount of retrieval database (about 0.35%) and training data (about 11%) can show even better adaptability compared to models that are trained on larger datasets.

generalization_capability.value: StepSearch exhibits robust generalization, particularly in smaller-scale models, showing better performance than strong baseline models across different sizes and types.
generalization_capability.evidence: StepSearch exhibits robust generalization, particularly in smaller-scale models. Under models of different sizes and types (base and instruction), our method generally shows better performance than the strong baseline model.

ablation_study_findings.value: Redundancy penalty alone does not optimize search ability but can force high-quality, low-repetition effective search when applying information gain calculation. Searching keyword reward values improve convergence speed but may lead to hallucination and reward collapse problems.
ablation_study_findings.evidence: (2) Redundancy penalty alone does not optimize the search ability, but it can force the model to perform high-quality, low-repetition effective search when applying information gain calculation, thereby achieving a higher capacity ceiling; (3) Searching keyword reward values can significantly improve the model convergence speed, but without process supervision, it may lead to hallucination and reward collapse problems more quickly, causing the model to respond incoherently and fail to converge;

key_innovations.value: StepSearch integrates iterative retrieval with explicit step-wise supervision, enhancing retrieval fidelity and QA accuracy through token-level rewards combining information gain and redundancy penalties.
key_innovations.evidence: By augmenting PPO with token-level rewards that combine information gain and redundancy penalties, StepSearch boosts policy convergence and improves retrieval fidelity and QA accuracy.

method_comparison.value: StepSearch outperforms standard RL baselines by 5.7%, 9.1%, 10.0%, and 15.2% absolutely on diverse multi-hop QA benchmarks.
method_comparison.evidence: StepSearch outperforms standard RL baselines by 5.7%, 9.1%, 10.0%, and 15.2% absolutely on diverse multi-hop QA benchmarks.

case_study_example.value: The case study demonstrates StepSearch's effectiveness in accurately identifying the educational institution that publishes the Quarterly named after the state where Eastwood Park Historic District is located.
case_study_example.evidence: The search results provide information about the North Dakota Quarterly, which is a quarterly literary journal published by the University of North Dakota. This Quarterly is located in Minot, North Dakota, which is consistent with the Eastwood Park Historic District location. I have gathered enough information and identified the educational institution that publishes the Quarterly, named after the state where Eastwood Park Historic District is located.

current_limitations.value: Evaluation has been restricted to text-only question answering, leaving open the question of generalization to multimodal inputs and tasks that cross paradigmatic boundaries. Scaling to larger models may exacerbate issues such as reward collapse and unstable training dynamics.
current_limitations.evidence: Despite the advances demonstrated by our retrieval-augmented reasoning framework, it remains subject to several important limitations. Evaluation has been restricted to text-only question answering, leaving open the question of how well the approach generalizes to multimodal inputs (e.g., images, audio) and to tasks that cross paradigmatic boundaries. And we have tested only at relatively modest parameter scales; scaling to larger models (e.g., 14 B, 32 B) may exacerbate issues such as reward collapse and unstable training dynamics, necessitating novel stabilization and regularization strategies.

future_research_directions.value: Enhance post-retrieval comprehension and metacognitive reflection, enrich the diversity and complexity of search behaviors, extend framework beyond text-only QA to multimodal, cross-paradigm setting, and explore scaling StePPO to larger parameter regimes.
future_research_directions.evidence: Looking ahead, we plan to enhance the agent’s post-retrieval comprehension and metacognitive reflection, enabling more nuanced integration of external evidence; enrich the diversity and complexity of search behaviors to better mirror real-world information-seeking dynamics; and extend our framework beyond text-only QA to a truly multimodal, cross-paradigm setting. Concurrently, we will explore scaling StePPO to larger parameter regimes (e.g., 14 B and 32 B) and develop robust countermeasures against reward collapse to ensure stable, efficient training at scale.

key_contribution.value: StepSearch enhances retrieval fidelity and QA accuracy through token-level rewards combining information gain and redundancy penalties, demonstrating robust generalization and adaptability to out-of-domain knowledge bases.
key_contribution.evidence: By augmenting PPO with token-level rewards that combine information gain and redundancy penalties, StepSearch boosts policy convergence and improves retrieval fidelity and QA accuracy.

broader_implications.value: The research impacts the development of AI systems by demonstrating a novel approach to optimizing search-based document retrieval in complex multi-hop reasoning tasks.
broader_implications.evidence: Efficient multi-hop reasoning requires Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents to acquire high-value external knowledge iteratively.
